name: REVELATION BIOSCIENCES, INC.
business: Revelation is a clinical-stage life science company focused on rebalancing inflammation to optimize health using its proprietary formulation Gemini. The company is developing GEM-AKI as a treatment for acute kidney injury (AKI) and GEM-CKD as a treatment for chronic kidney disease (CKD).

exposures.sentence: Gemini is our proprietary formulation platform of phosphorylated hexaacyl disaccharide (PHAD), an established Toll-like receptor 4 (TLR4) agonist that works by modulating and rebalancing a dysregulated inflammatory response.

catalysts.event: FDA agreement on single Phase 2/3 adaptive design study for GEM-AKI
catalysts.sentence: Revelation met with FDA at the end of 2025 and obtained agreement on a single Phase 2/3 adaptive design study with a primary composite endpoint of death and/or dialysis for submission of a new drug application (NDA).

catalysts.event: Plan to build infrastructure and start Phase 2/3 study for GEM-AKI
catalysts.sentence: During 2026, the Company plans to build the necessary infrastructure to successfully run this clinical study including engagement of a scientific advisory board, engagement of a top-tier clinical research organization, and manufacture of blinded drug and placebo with the intention of starting the Phase 2/3 study as soon as feasible.

catalysts.event: Plan to conduct preclinical toxicology studies for GEM-CKD
catalysts.sentence: During 2026, Revelation intends to conduct preclinical toxicology studies to support a repeat dose Phase 2 study to allow further evaluation of Gemini in this high-need patient population.

tamClaims.claim: AKI affects more than 10% of all hospitalized patients and more than 50% of patients admitted to intensive care units.
tamClaims.sentence: AKI is a major cause of morbidity and mortality, affecting more than 10% of all hospitalized patients and more than 50% of patients admitted to intensive care units.

tamClaims.claim: Approximately 6.8 million patients admitted to US hospitals had AKI annually.
tamClaims.sentence: It was found that 20% or approximately 6.8 million patients admitted to hospitals had AKI by the University of Florida.

tamClaims.claim: Medicare annual expenditure for AKI was over $10 billion and growing, with per-patient costs of approximately $42,077.
tamClaims.sentence: The CDC says Medicare in 2015 alone had an annual expenditure of over $10 billion and growing for AKI, with spending for AKI related costs of approximately $42,077 per patient.

tamClaims.claim: CKD affects 15% of US adults, or 37 million people.
tamClaims.sentence: According to the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (the CDC ), more than 1 in 7, that is 15%, of US adults or 37 million people are estimated to have CKD.

tamClaims.claim: Medicare costs for CKD are estimated in excess of $87 billion.
tamClaims.sentence: The CDC estimates Medicare costs in excess of $87 billion and continues to promote reduced costs including better management of CKD.
